one_time_description: Gain from property lost in fire
economic_notes: First Nation operating under co-management arrangement per ISC directives. COVID-19 impacted operations with periodic closures. Holds one unit in Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership (OFNLP) receiving 1.7% of aggregate provincial gross gaming revenues. Sewage pumping station contract signed for $2,061,464.
notes: Audit is qualified due to omission of budget comparison information (not a misstatement qualification). Going concern note present due to co-management arrangement with ISC, but no formal going concern conclusion. Fiscal year is March 31, 2021. Auditor firm name not clearly identified in the document (signed in Timmins, Ontario). TCA net book value not extractable from available text (Schedule 1 referenced but not fully transcribed). Social services expenses included within Community Health segment. OFNLP accounted for as restricted accumulated surplus rather than equity-method GBE. ISC funding calculated as fixed + set + grant contributions; FNIHB included in total government transfers. Net debt of $912,938 at year end (improved from $4,215,639 prior year). Drinking water settlement eligibility noted as contingency.
